skill.name: scite-database
skill.description: Access Scite.ai Smart Citations to classify how a paper is cited (supporting, contrasting, mentioning) and assess scientific claims; use it when you need to evaluate a paper's reliability or its acceptance in the literature.
skill.summary: Query Scite.ai citation classification data for any DOI to assess how a paper is received in the literature.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: The scite-database skill provides legitimate access to Scite.ai Smart Citation data through a Python HTTP client. All 48 static analysis findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives: 13 'weak cryptographic algorithm' flags are triggered by documentation text and JSON metadata (no cryptographic code exists in the skill), 27 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are Markdown inline code and fenced bash blocks in SKILL.md (no Ruby code present), and 5 'system reconnaissance' flags come from evaluation metadata JSON. The only genuine risk factor is network access, which is expected and necessary for an API client that queries api.scite.ai. A minor observation is the use of a browser-impersonating User-Agent header, which is common practice but noted for transparency.

low_findings.title: Browser User-Agent Impersonation
low_findings.description: The Python client uses a Chrome browser User-Agent string (Mozilla/5.0 Windows NT 10.0...) instead of a descriptive application identifier. While common in practice, this bypasses API access controls designed for programmatic clients and may not comply with the Scite API terms of service.

content.user_title: Analyze Smart Citations to Assess Scientific Claims
content.value_statement: Evaluating a research paper's reliability requires understanding how it was cited by later work. This skill automates access to Scite.ai Smart Citations data, classifying citations as supporting, contrasting, or mentioning to help you quickly gauge consensus and controversy around any DOI.
actual_capabilities: Query citation classification data for any valid DOI from Scite.ai
Return counts of supporting, contrasting, and mentioning citations
Output results as formatted text for quick inspection
Output results as JSON for pipeline integration
Display venue or journal metadata when available from the API
limitations: Requires a valid DOI and stable internet access to api.scite.ai
Returns only aggregate citation counts, not full citation context or snippets
Public Scite API availability and response structure may change without notice
Does not support batch DOI lookups in a single query

use_cases.title: Evaluate Paper Credibility
use_cases.description: Assess whether a research paper is mostly supported or frequently contradicted by later citations before citing it in your own work.

use_cases.title: Prioritize Literature Review Reading
use_cases.description: Quickly gauge which papers in a reading list have strong consensus versus significant controversy using citation classification data.

use_cases.title: Fact-Check Scientific Statements
use_cases.description: Validate whether a scientific claim is broadly supported or disputed in subsequent publications by examining citation sentiment profiles.

prompt_templates.title: Single DOI Citation Lookup
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the scite-database skill to analyze citations for DOI 10.1038/nature12345. Show me the supporting, contrasting, and mentioning citation counts.

prompt_templates.title: JSON Format for Data Pipelines
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the scite-database skill to retrieve citation data for DOI 10.1038/nature12345. Return the results in JSON format so I can process them further.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Two Papers
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to compare the citation reception of two papers. Use the scite-database skill to analyze DOI 10.1038/nature12345 and DOI 10.1126/science.1258096. Compare their supporting, contrasting, and mentioning citation counts.

prompt_templates.title: Full Evidence Assessment with Verification
prompt_templates.prompt: Retrieve citation classification data for DOI 10.1038/nature12345 using the scite-database skill. Summarize the results and assess whether the paper claims appear well-supported or controversial based on the citation profile. Note any caveats about API data completeness.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced analytical workflow for evidence synthesis

output_examples.input: Use the scite-database skill to analyze DOI 10.1038/nature12345.
output_examples.output:
--- Scite Analysis for 10.1038/nature12345 ---
Total Citations: 45
Supporting:      12
Contrasting:     1
Mentioning:      32

output_examples.input: Get citation data for DOI 10.1038/nature12345 in JSON format.

best_practices: Verify the DOI format before running a query to avoid API errors and unnecessary requests.
Use JSON output format when integrating results into automated pipelines or downstream analysis tools.
Cross-reference citation classification results with the original paper context to understand the nuance behind each citation category.
anti_patterns: Do not use citation classification data as the sole measure of a paper quality; supplement with full-text reading and peer review.
Do not assume the Scite API data is complete or real-time; check the data freshness before drawing conclusions.
Do not bypass error handling by removing the proxy or timeout parameters, especially in restricted network environments.

faq.question: What is a DOI and where do I find one?
faq.answer: A DOI (Digital Object Identifier) is a unique identifier for academic papers. It is usually printed on the first page of a paper or listed in database entries. Example: 10.1038/nature12345.

faq.question: What do supporting, contrasting, and mentioning citations mean?
faq.answer: Supporting citations agree with the paper claims. Contrasting citations disagree or present conflicting evidence. Mentioning citations reference the paper without taking a position on its claims.

faq.question: Does this skill work without an internet connection?
faq.answer: No. The skill queries the Scite.ai API over the internet and requires a stable connection to api.scite.ai.

faq.question: Can I analyze multiple DOIs at once?
faq.answer: The current version processes one DOI per query. For multiple DOIs, run separate queries for each identifier.

faq.question: Is the Scite API data free to use?
faq.answer: The public Scite API used by this skill may have usage limits. Check Scite.ai terms of service for current rate limits and access policies.

faq.question: How current is the citation data returned by this skill?
faq.answer: The data freshness depends on the Scite.ai API. Citation counts may not include the most recently published works. Always verify timeliness for time-sensitive analyses.
